div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in the Soviet Union (Steinhardt)
div.p: 54. Your 89, January 22, noon. As explained to the Soviet Ambassador on January 18: (1) The Army and Navy have the right to decide whether aliens will be permitted in plants manufacturing equipment for national defence; (2) The Wright plant is manufacturing such equipment and the Army and Navy requested that visits of foreigners to the plant be permitted only when necessary for the purchase of equipment; (3) The Soviet engineers excluded were not engaged in purchasing equipment; and (4) The Wright contract contained a provision to the effect that visits of Soviet technicians to the plant might be refused if disapproved by the United States Government.
A copy of memorandum of conversation will be transmitted by pouch.Missing from Department files.
